content: WebSocket relay architecture uses three tiers: a cloud relay server (broker), an outbound-connecting daemon on user machines, and browser clients. The daemon initiates persistent outbound connections (solving NAT/firewall without port-forwarding). Browser clients also connect to the relay. Commands route via the relay using a connection registry keyed on device/session ID. Messages are addressed to a specific device connection ID; the relay looks up which server instance holds that connection and forwards. For multi-server setups, a pub/sub backplane (Redis, NATS) routes messages to the correct instance.
